pr.title: grbigblueroute: match C85C, DED4, DF80 sizes; improve CD20 and fix sdata2 ordering
pr.author: kolt-mcb

summary: Closed, unmerged PR touching only src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.c. It attempted stage decomp matching improvements for grBigBlueRoute functions C85C, CD20, DED4, and DF80, plus .sdata2 constant-order work. The PR body claimed exact size matches for C85C, DED4, and DF80 and a 4-byte CD20 gap, but the author immediately commented that an agent made the PR before it was ready, so claimed results should be treated as provisional rather than accepted.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains all function-size, control-flow, register-pressure, and .sdata2-ordering edits in the PR.

what_changed.text: Updated grBigBlueRoute_8020C85C by replacing a negated less-than check with an equivalent greater-than-or-equal early return: `if (gp->u.car.x108 >= 1.0f + grBb_Route_804D6A68->x40) return;`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: C85C change says invert comparison pattern to match target size

what_changed.text: Updated grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 declaration order and local layout around `root_jobj`, `jobj`, and `i`; retained `volatile Vec3 rot` and PAD_STACK(0x4C).
In CD20 case 0, moved spline point, tangent, vector diff, and rotation setup before xC adjustment, then used the adjusted fraction to scale and add position.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CD20 case 0 hunk moving splGetSplinePoint/lbShadow_8000E9F0/lbVector_Diff/atan2f before xC adjustment

what_changed.text: Inserted inline discarded `45.0f * Ground_801C0498();` calls to influence .sdata2 constant ordering without extending a variable live range, according to the PR body.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: .sdata2 constants section and CD20 changes

what_changed.text: Changed a jobj update from a ternary expression to explicit if/else around `grBigBlueRoute_8020DA9C`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CD20 end-of-loop jobj ternary replaced with if/else
human_pr_text: CD20 change says change jobj ternary to if-else to match target control flow

what_changed.text: Reworked grBigBlueRoute_8020DED4 from apparent clamp assignments into repeated `Ground_801C0498()` and `Ground_801C38BC()` calls.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: DED4 change says restructure to use Ground_801C0498() + Ground_801C38BC() pattern

what_changed.text: Changed grBigBlueRoute_8020DF80 condition from `a->y > b.y + -3.0F` to `b.y > -3.0f`, and added `Ground_801C38BC(a->y, b.y)` in the false branch.

Updated a CD20 todo comment from an 89.41% match note to a specific 3448/3452-byte, 4-byte-gap register-allocation note mentioning `stmw r24` versus target `stmw r25`, stack size 592B, FPR spills f16-f31, and .sdata2 [0-22] constants.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Known gap and .sdata2 constants sections

smart_moves.text: The PR documented the unresolved CD20 gap with a narrow suspected codegen cause instead of claiming a full match: extra GPR allocation in the prologue, same stack frame size, same FPR spill range, and shifted register allocation.
The PR body separated exact-size functions from the still-gapped CD20 function, making the claimed status searchable by function.
The author explicitly flagged the PR as not ready in an issue comment, which is useful intake evidence for not promoting the claimed matches as accepted.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment by kolt-mcb: sorry my agent made this pr before it was ready

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a near-match differs by a small prologue/register-save gap, record the exact register-save difference, stack frame size, FPR spill range, and tested failed tactics rather than presenting the function as fully matched.
Equivalent source-level control-flow forms can affect target size and branch shape; this PR tried comparison inversion for C85C and if/else instead of ternary for CD20.

Constant ordering work in a code-matching PR is risky when it depends on discarded computations; this PR is evidence of the tactic being attempted, not evidence that the tactic should be accepted as style.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready

naming_conventions.text: The diff retains address-style function names such as grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20, grBigBlueRoute_8020DED4, and grBigBlueRoute_8020DF80; no new semantic global renames are evidenced.
Local names are role-based and short where visible: `gp`, `gobj`, `jobj`, `root_jobj`, `fighter`, `fighter_pos`, `pos`, `rot`, `p0`, `p1`, `tangent`, `road_tan`, and `road`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 declaration and local blocks

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses `RE_ENTRY` as a macro alias for the indexed route entry: `#define RE_ENTRY (&((RouteEntry*) gp->u.car.car_info)[i])`; this is existing context in the diff, not evidence of a new global naming standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration/local-order adjustments for register pressure and codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: CD20 moved `root_jobj`, `jobj`, and `i` declarations after Vec3 locals. The PR body says declaration-order tests failed to close the 4-byte GPR gap.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Known gap says declaration order tests failed

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Move computations earlier to affect live ranges/control flow.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: CD20 moved spline/tangent/rotation computations before xC adjustment in case 0.
CD20 changed the jobj update at loop end from a conditional expression to explicit if/else, with the PR body saying this matched target control flow.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Discarded expression to perturb .sdata2 constant ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR inserted `45.0f * Ground_801C0498();` and states it was intended to fix .sdata2 ordering without a variable live range. Because the PR was not ready, this should remain a reviewed tactic candidate, not a style precedent.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR body reports declaration order, block scoping, volatile variations, ternary vs if-else patterns, and 90k+ decomp-permuter iterations did not reduce CD20 register pressure.

review_feedback.author: kolt-mcb
review_feedback.text: sorry my agent made this pr before it was ready.

follow_up_queries.query: Find later PRs touching grBigBlueRoute_8020CD20 or grbigblueroute.c after PR #2695 to see whether the 4-byte CD20 gap was resolved.
follow_up_queries.reason: This PR was closed unmerged and self-marked not ready.

follow_up_queries.query: Search for `stmw r24` `stmw r25` `CD20` in Big Blue route PRs or comments.
follow_up_queries.reason: The exact unresolved gap signature is likely reusable for follow-up matching work.

follow_up_queries.query: Search for discarded `45.0f * Ground_801C0498()` or similar discarded Ground_801C0498 multiplications.
follow_up_queries.reason: This PR attempted the pattern for .sdata2 ordering; curator should verify whether later accepted source kept or rejected it.

follow_up_queries.query: Search later history for grBigBlueRoute_8020DED4 and Ground_801C38BC/Ground_801C0498 pattern.
follow_up_queries.reason: The DED4 rewrite is unusual and the PR was not ready, so later accepted evidence is needed before learning a standard.

notes: Strong evidence exists for what file changed and what edits were proposed because the raw diff is available.
The function-size and .sdata2-match claims come from the PR author/body; no independent build, objdiff, or checkdiff output is included in the slice.

The PR was closed with merged_at null and the author immediately commented that the agent made the PR before it was ready; therefore claimed matches and tactics should not be treated as accepted project knowledge.
No review comments or reviewer approvals are present in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record exact unresolved register-allocation gaps instead of overstating near matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2695 documents CD20 as 3448/3452 bytes with the suspected prologue difference `stmw r24` vs target `stmw r25`, matching 592B stack frame, matching f16-f31 FPR spills, and failed attempts including declaration order, block scoping, volatile variations, ternary vs if-else, and 90k+ permuter iterations. This is useful as a search pattern and as an example of honest unresolved-gap documentation, but not as proof of a merged match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was not ready

source_update_candidates.title: Treat discarded computations for data-section ordering as high-risk matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2695 inserted a discarded `45.0f * Ground_801C0498();` expression and described it as fixing .sdata2 constant ordering without a variable live range. Because the PR was closed unmerged and self-marked not ready, this should not become accepted style; if promoted at all, it should reinforce that data-order perturbation tactics need explicit verifier evidence and cleanup review.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already warn against unsupported matching tactics and data-section chasing; this PR provides a concrete historical example for curator review, not a new accepted rule.

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote the PR body's exact size-match claims as accepted validation results; the slice lacks independent verifier output and the PR was explicitly marked not ready by its author.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote the DED4 Ground_801C0498/Ground_801C38BC rewrite as a general clamp style; the resulting source shape is unusual, and the PR readiness comment weakens it as evidence.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready

rejection_notes.text: Do not treat discarded `45.0f * Ground_801C0498()` as an accepted method for .sdata2 ordering without later merged evidence.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: issue_comment says PR was made before ready
